channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tropical Depression Haishen Develops East of Philippines, Still Outside PAR
item.description: A low-pressure area tracked by PAGASA strengthened into a tropical depression named Haishen on July 13, 2026. As of 8 a.m., Haishen was located 1,275 kilometers east of Eastern Visayas with maximum su...

item.title: Linda Mwananchi's Movement Reshapes Kenyan Politics
item.description: The article explores how Linda Mwananchi has emerged as a transformative political force in Kenya, uniting generations through shared ideals rather than personal loyalty. It challenges traditional pow...

item.title: Sudan court sentences RSF leader Hemeti to death for war crimes
item.description: A Sudanese court has sentenced Mohamed Hamdan 'Hemeti' Dagalo, the leader of the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), and 15 others to death in absentia for their roles in killing regional governor Khamis Abba...

item.title: FG closes illegal mining site in Osun after arresting suspects
item.description: The Federal Government (FG) has taken action against illegal mining activities in Osun State by arresting two suspects and shutting down the affected mining site. The operation, carried out by federal...

item.title: Regina Roughriders dominate Ti-Cats with 38-7 victory
item.description: The Regina Roughriders delivered a commanding performance in their matchup against the Ti-Cats, overcoming a sluggish offensive start to secure a decisive 38-7 win. The game, played on July 12, 2026, ...

item.title: Central US Experiences Record-Breaking Heatwave
item.description: A severe heatwave has broken temperature records across the central United States, prompting widespread concerns about public health and infrastructure resilience. The unprecedented weather event, cha...

item.title: Nigerian universities expand captive power generation as unreliable national grid pushes institutions towards self-generation
item.description: Nigerian universities are increasingly investing in their own electricity generation as the country's persistent power supply challenges continue to affect teaching, research and daily campus operatio...

item.title: Prime Minister Carney Promotes Pipeline Development Amid Alberta Energy Discussions
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's chief of staff, David Carney, addressed energy policy and pipeline development during his appearance at the Calgary Stampede, emphasizing collaboration across provinces...

item.title: Heatwaves in the UK May Have Led to Thousands of Deaths
item.description: Experts estimate that over 2,700 people may have died from heat-related causes during the UK's exceptionally hot weather in May and June. The figures, from Imperial College London, the Met Office, and...

item.title: US-Iran Tensions Escalate with Strikes and Retaliation Over Hormuz Control
item.description: The United States and Iran are engaged in a renewed military confrontation over control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz, with both sides launching coordinated strikes and retaliatory attacks. US for...

item.title: Heritage Western Cape halts Oude Molen Eco Village redevelopment due to non-compliant heritage assessment
item.description: The Heritage Western Cape Appeals Committee has rejected the provincial government's appeal against the refusal of the Heritage Impact Assessment (HIA) for the Oude Molen Eco Village redevelopment. Th...

item.title: PM Shehbaz's condolences to Qatar on former emir Sheikh Hamad's passing
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has embarked on a one-day visit to Qatar to express condolences for the death of former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who passed away at 74. The visit includes ...

item.title: Man Who Regained Consciousness After Being Declared Dead Shares His Experience
item.description: Gene Long, a 33-year-old man with a history of severe health issues including diabetes, addiction, and homelessness, was declared dead after a catastrophic medical emergency. His family had already st...

item.title: Meta Removes AI Feature on Instagram Following User Backlash
item.description: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram after facing significant backlash from users and stakeholders. The feature, which allowed users to modify photos from public Instagram accoun...

item.title: 5 Summer Organizing Projects to Streamline Your Routine
item.description: This article provides practical tips for managing summer chaos through strategic organizing projects. The author highlights five key areas to address: 1) Taming the craft-supply explosion by sorting a...

item.title: Annie Lord discusses modern dating, her debut novel and the impact of dating apps on contemporary relationships
item.description: Writer Annie Lord reflects on the realities of modern dating, the influence of dating apps and her transition from memoir and journalism to fiction with the publication of her debut novel, *The Projec...

item.title: British woman dies from injuries after southern Spain wildfire, raising death toll to 13
item.description: A 93-year-old British woman has died in hospital after suffering severe burns during the devastating wildfire that swept through the Los Gallardos area of Almería in southern Spain. Her death raises t...

item.title: US strikes in Iran kill one, injure four; oil prices surge
item.description: The US military conducted fresh strikes in Iran's Khuzestan province, killing one person and injuring four others. The attacks targeted an agricultural water pumping station, according to Anadolu Agen...

item.title: LRT2 Now Accepts Visa and Mastercard Payments for Smoother Commuting
item.description: Commuters in the Philippines can now use contactless payments via Visa or Mastercard to pay for their Light Rail Transit Line 2 (LRT2) rides. This update, announced by Visa and Mastercard on July 13, ...

item.title: South African Man Smuggled 17.5kg Heroin Hidden in Coffee and Tea Bags, Arrested in Thailand
item.description: Thailand's Customs Department has arrested a South African man for attempting to smuggle 17.5 kilograms of heroin hidden in coffee and green tea bags. The arrest occurred on July 9, 2026, when officia...
